.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:364:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer on the Efficacy page, I understand what Compare is for the moment I see it, and I can either select rows I already care about and compare them, or I can ignore the feature entirely if it's not for me.\n\n**Acceptance criteria (assume outcome (2) — \"redesign in place\"; rewrite these if (1) or (3) is chosen):**\n\n1. **Decision note.** One-pager in `docs/helix/02-design/` records the chosen outcome (relocate / redesign / remove), the evidence behind it (usage grep, dependency scan), and the scope of remaining work. Implementation PR links to it.\n\n2. **Row-selection entry point.** Efficacy table rows gain a leading checkbox column. Selecting ≥2 rows enables a \"Compare selected\" button in the page header. With 0 or 1 rows selected the button is disabled (not hidden) with a tooltip explaining why.\n\n3. **Pre-seeded comparison.** Clicking \"Compare selected\" launches the existing comparison flow with arms pre-populated from the selected rows' `(harness, provider, model)` tuples. The prompt field is still required (comparisons need a prompt) but is the only field the user has to fill in — the arm list is done.\n\n4. **Removed from cold-start.** The standalone \"Compare\" button on the Efficacy page (the one that creates an empty comparison) is removed. New comparisons originate from a row selection. If users legitimately want a from-scratch comparison, that path lives on a separate `/experiments` page (out of scope for this bead unless the design note calls for it).\n\n5. **Empty-state copy.** When the Efficacy table is empty, the Compare affordance is hidden entirely (no point selecting zero rows).\n\n6. **Playwright e2e.**\n   - Seeds the fixture with ≥3 efficacy rows.\n   - Asserts the Compare button is disabled with 0/1 selections and enabled with 2+.\n   - Selects 2 rows, clicks Compare, asserts the comparison form opens with arms pre-populated from those rows and only the prompt field is empty.\n\n7. **No regression to efficacy perf.** All perf targets from `ddx-0a33bc5f` still hold. Adding a checkbox column does not change the payload the resolver returns.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-24T06:34:30Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"196235","closing_commit_sha":"7db5cc9c31bb89b506ba5716148976f904db2aba","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:47:38.842277927Z","description":"## Observed\n\nThe Efficacy page has a \"Compare\" button that opens a flow for adding \"arms\" and writing a prompt. User feedback: they're not sure what to do with it. It does not match their mental model of what efficacy is (a session aggregation grouped by provider / harness / model).\n\n## Ground truth\n\n- The backing feature is `Query.comparisons` and the comparison record set (`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_feat008.go:211-226`). It's an A/B-style \"run the same prompt across N arms and compare results\" tool. That is a real capability — just not what a rollup/efficacy dashboard is for.\n- It's colocated with Efficacy today probably because both involve harness/model performance. The colocation is creating confusion: the Efficacy page promises \"how are things doing\" and the Compare button promises \"design an experiment\" — different audiences, different tasks.\n\n## Decision required\n\nBefore implementation, decide one of three:\n\n1. **Relocate.** Move Compare to its own top-level surface (e.g., `/experiments` or `/compare`) and cleanly separate \"how did things go\" (efficacy) from \"design an A/B\" (compare). Efficacy page loses the Compare button entirely.\n2. **Redesign in place.** Keep it on the Efficacy page but recast as \"Compare these rows\" — the user selects 2+ efficacy rows and launches a comparison pre-seeded from them. Removes the cold-start prompt-entry UX; connects directly to the rollup the user is already looking at.\n3. **Remove.** If comparisons are not being used by real workflows, drop the feature and the associated UI/resolver. Least code, least surface area.\n\nThe user did not mandate a specific choice — they flagged the confusion and the mental model. Option 2 aligns best with their stated mental model (efficacy = session aggregation); option 1 is the cleanest separation of concerns; option 3 is viable only if usage data supports it.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nRun the design decision as a short spike (≤ half a day):\n\n- Check whether `comparisons` is referenced by anything beyond this UI page: CLI, docs, specs, other server endpoints. Deletion cost is a function of that.\n- Usage check: grep agent-logs + bead evidence for any `comparison_id` or `compare` correlation over the last 30 days. Is anyone actually running comparisons?\n- Write a one-page decision note in `docs/helix/02-design/` recording the choice, the evidence that supports it, and the scope of the implementation work.\n\nThis bead's remaining acceptance criteria are written assuming the outcome is (2) \"redesign in place\" — the most-likely-to-be-built option. They should be rewritten when the decision is made if the outcome is (1) or (3).\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Anything the Efficacy perf/source rework (`ddx-0a33bc5f`) covers.\n- Multi-run orchestration changes to how comparisons execute.","events_attachment":"ddx-6ce6d7a7/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-24T06:39:30.288516189Z","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model \"minimax/minimax-m2.7\" for harness \"gemini\"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-23T03:02:04Z","feature":"FEAT-019","id":"ddx-6ce6d7a7","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-008","feat-019","efficacy","ui","design"],"owner":"ddx","priority":3,"session_id":"eb-1ea62cda","status":"closed","title":"Efficacy 'Compare arms' button is unclear and misplaced: decide whether to relocate, redesign, or remove","updated_at":"2026-04-24T06:40:37.909246767Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:382:{"acceptance":"1. ddx work --model qwen3.6-35b-a3b routes to a discovered LM Studio provider (vidar/bragi) without --harness or --provider.\n2. ddx work --model nonexistent-model-xyz errors: \"model X is not in the catalog and no discovered provider serves it\".\n3. Profile routing (--profile cheap) does NOT auto-select uncataloged discovered models.\n4. Partial discovery: when one provider times out, routing succeeds with responding providers.\n5. Discovery cache: repeated routing within 30s reuses cached /v1/models results.\n\nUnit tests:\nT1. Exact model ID found on discovered provider → viable candidate plan created.\nT2. Model not in catalog and not discovered → clear error (not silent fallback).\nT3. Profile routing ignores uncataloged discovered models.\nT4. Partial failure: one provider unreachable, another serves model → routes to available one.\nT5. Cache returns same results within TTL without re-probing.\n\ncd cli \u0026\u0026 go test ./... \u0026\u0026 go build .","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-17T19:21:08.654084559Z","description":"Wire /v1/models discovery from configured providers into BuildCandidatePlans\nso that --model with an exact model ID routes to a discovered provider\nwithout requiring --harness or --provider.\n\nThis is the foundation layer — fuzzy matching (bead B) and routing mode\ncleanup (bead C) depend on this.\n\nImplementation:\n- Add a DiscoverModels function that loads agentconfig, probes each provider's\n  /v1/models endpoint (reusing providerstatus.Probe), and returns a map of\n  provider→[]modelID.\n- Cache results per-process with a 30s TTL.\n- On partial failure (some providers timeout), proceed with responding providers.\n- In BuildCandidatePlans (or a new variant), when processing a ModelPin that\n  no harness can serve via catalog, consult discovered models to find providers\n  that advertise the pin. Create candidate plans for those provider+model pairs\n  mapped to the appropriate harness surface.\n- Remove or bypass ValidateOrphanModel for models found via discovery.\n- Discovered-only models are assumed high-cost/low-performance for scoring\n  (they don't win profile routing, only explicit --model).","id":"ddx-71fe194f","issue_type":"task","priority":1,"spec-id":"SD-015","status":"closed","title":"Wire live provider discovery into candidate planning","updated_at":"2026-04-17T19:40:23.30337834Z"}

Repro fixture bead-closed-no-evidence.jsonl (from ddx-004eccbc) replays through the execute-loop close path and is rejected — the bead does not transition to closed.\n3. No new invariant machinery here — the closure invariant bead (ddx-e30e60a9) owns cross-path enforcement; this bead owns the execute-loop symptom repro.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-20T13:56:00.206769663Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-738edf47","depends_on_id":"ddx-e30e60a9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:03:56Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-738edf47","depends_on_id":"ddx-004eccbc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:04:11Z"}],"description":"Reporter: nexiq+axon 2026-04-18/19/20. Symptom 3 of review-malfunction report. Most damaging of the three report findings because it hides incomplete work.\n\nObserved on axon `axon-c5cc071a` (RBAC field/row/entity spec, ~2000-word AC) 2026-04-19:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"id\": \"axon-c5cc071a\",\n  \"claimed-at\": \"2026-04-19T23:20:45Z\",\n  \"claimed-machine\": \"sindri\",\n  \"claimed-pid\": \"3479503\",\n  \"execute-loop-heartbeat-at\": \"2026-04-19T23:20:45.364638776Z\",\n  \"updated_at\": \"2026-04-19T23:25:18.98959532Z\",\n  \"status\": \"closed\",\n  \"events\": []\n}\n```\n\n4m33s from claim to closed, `events: []`, no execution bundle under `.ddx/executions/` referencing the bead, no git commit referencing the id, no branch, no notes. Reopened 2026-04-20 as `axon-c5cc071a`. Evidence this is worker-side: no other actor claimed the bead, `claimed-pid` matches execute-loop PID pattern, `execute-loop-heartbeat-at` was set at claim — a manual `ddx bead update` would not leave `events: []` while setting `status: closed`.\n\nRoot-cause hypothesis from report: the worker's cleanup path closes the bead on primary-task success even when the reviewer sub-step errors out without returning a parseable verdict (e.g. context-overflow `n_keep: 1,868,459 \u003e= n_ctx: 196,608` seen in reporter's LLM server logs, or any exception in the review state-machine path). A `bead.result` event that never gets emitted still leaves the bead closed if the exit path is not gated on verdict presence.\n\nClosure contract should be: a bead transitions to `status=closed` only when a valid terminal review verdict (APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES / BLOCK / SKIP) is recorded alongside an execution bundle and at least one bead event. Worker crash, malformed reviewer output, or context-overflow during review must transition the bead back to `in_progress` with a retry marker — never to `closed`.\n\nPrior related bead `ddx-b57be3ab` (closed) addressed BLOCK-with-empty-rationale but treated the rationale-missing case as `review_malfunction` without closing; this report shows a separate path where the closure itself happens despite reviewer failure.","id":"ddx-738edf47","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:exec","area:bead","kind:bug","kind:correctness","goal:throughput-per-dollar","wave:2","workstream:reviewer"],"priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"exec: bead closes without valid reviewer verdict — silent false-closure hides unfinished work","updated_at":"2026-04-20T16:23:03.738432327Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:399:{"archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-15T06:24:35Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"3766264","closing_commit_sha":"604f17d53ce26b5776472f9fa77a82a2eb1b9cba","created_at":"2026-04-15T01:35:08.058474818Z","description":"The ddx-bead skill guides bead creation but underspecifies what makes a bead *execution-ready* for automated execute-bead runs. Observed during axon ADR-018 execute-loop driving: beads that technically met the current skill's requirements (title, AC, labels, description pointing at spec-id) still produced bad execute-bead outcomes because the description lacked the context the agent needed to ship correct code in one pass.\n\n## Gaps in the current skill\n\n1. **Description quality is underspecified.** The skill gives AC quality rules but the description field just says 'per SD-004 Section 2' is OK. In practice, agents need:\n   - Exact file paths to touch (e.g., 'crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs')\n   - Cross-bead context when dependencies are listed (e.g., 'A1 landed the credential_revocations table in crates/axon-storage/src/auth_schema.rs — query it via SELECT 1')\n   - Inline spec excerpts, NOT just doc-id references. If the spec is ADR-018 §4's failure table, paste the table into the description. Don't say 'see ADR-018 §4'.\n   - Files NOT to touch (scope negation)\n\n2. **spec-id and the doc graph are unreliable for execution context.** The current skill implies spec-id linkage is sufficient. It is not — spec-id drives docgraph.BuildGraphWithConfig() which only resolves HELIX-shaped doc references. Generic beads on non-HELIX projects have no equivalent. AND even when resolution works, the agent only gets a file path, not the relevant section. The bead description is the only place that carries content the agent can rely on.\n\n3. **No guidance on cross-bead context.** When bead B depends on bead A, B's description should describe what A landed (file paths, type names, key APIs) — not assume the agent will reopen A's history. Automated execute-bead does not have that context; each bead is read cold.\n\n4. **No guidance on verification commands in AC.** AC items like 'unit tests green' are too vague. Should be 'cargo test -p axon-server green' or 'bun test passes in sdk/typescript'. Named commands.\n\n5. **No guidance on bead size.** A bead should be one unit of testable work, sized so a single agent pass can complete it. Too small is overhead; too large defeats review. Heuristic: if the AC lists more than ~12 checkboxes, consider splitting.\n\n6. **No guidance on incorporating review findings on reopen.** When a bead is reopened after a REQUEST_CHANGES review, the author should either (a) fold the review findings into the description for the next attempt to see, or (b) rely on the tool to thread review events into the prompt (a separate ddx bead — see ddx-a317208c). The skill is silent on this.\n\n7. **No template for description.** A recommended description template (Scope / Files to touch / Cross-bead context / Verification / Quality bar) would make good beads cheaper to author.\n\n## Scope\n\nUpdate skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md to add:\n\n- A 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section that codifies the points above\n- A 'Description template' with recommended sections: Scope, Files to touch, Files NOT to touch, Cross-bead context, Verification commands, Spec excerpts inline\n- A 'Bead sizing' section with the ~12-AC heuristic\n- A 'Review-driven reopen' section that tells authors to update the description in place when a bead is reopened after a review rejection\n- A 'Common anti-patterns' section enumerating the mistakes observed during the axon execute-loop:\n  - Listing doc IDs without inlining the relevant text\n  - Not naming file paths in the description\n  - AC items that are not verifiable by a named command\n  - Depending on unmerged work without explaining what it landed\n\nKeep the existing required-fields and label-conventions sections — they are correct.\n\n## Context\n\nObserved during axon ADR-018 execute-loop:\n- Bead A1 was well-sized but initially had scope creep in its description (audit_retention_policies, which belonged to F1). Agent added the scope-creep table faithfully.\n- Bead A2's initial description said 'UserIdentity { provider, subject, user_id }' — a typo carried over from an earlier ADR revision. Agent implemented 'subject' faithfully even though ADR-018 §3 said 'external_id'. The description was the contract the agent followed.\n- Bead B1 is being re-authored right now with a richer description (inline ADR-018 §4 failure table, exact file paths, cross-bead context about A1 schema and A2 types) — this is the experiment to validate the authoring changes proposed in this bead.\n\n## Acceptance\n\n- [ ] skills/ddx-bead/SKILL.md gains 'What makes a bead execution-ready' section\n- [ ] Description template added with the 6 recommended sections\n- [ ] Bead sizing heuristic documented\n- [ ] Review-driven reopen guidance added\n- [ ] Common anti-patterns enumerated\n- [ ] Skill still lints against any existing skill-format CI check","events_attachment":"ddx-78613f54/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-15T06:25:35.769449241Z","id":"ddx-78613f54","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","area:bead","area:skill","kind:documentation","gap:bead-authoring"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-4ef43076","status":"closed","title":"skill: improve ddx-bead SKILL.md with authoring discipline for execution-ready beads","updated_at":"2026-04-15T06:25:42.623027765Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:521:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer using the web UI on a real-world-sized workspace (tens of projects × hundreds of beads each), the beads list opens in under a second and the cross-project all-beads view stays usable. Before any perf fix lands, we can reproduce the problem and measure improvement — not guess.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Perf harness exists and is reusable.**\n   - Fixture builder generates `N × M` beads across projects with configurable shape (status mix, label density, dep count). Reuses the real `bead.Store` write path (no fake writer).\n   - Benchmark covers at minimum: `bead(id:)`, `beadsByProject`, `beads` (cross-project), `docGraph`, `sessions`. Adding a new target is a single-table-row change.\n   - Each target produces p50/p95/p99 for in-process calls and end-to-end HTTP. Results write to a human-readable report (markdown or JSON) plus a machine-readable artifact suitable for CI diffing later.\n\n2. **Baseline recorded.** First run of the harness on the current `main` binary is stored in the bead notes and in `docs/helix/04-observe/perf/YYYY-MM-DD-baseline.md` (or equivalent existing location). Numbers include hardware + go version footer.\n\n3. **BeadsByProject scope fix.** `BeadsByProject` calls a new `GetBeadSnapshotsForProject(projectID, …)` that opens exactly one project's store. Unit test asserts only that project's store is opened (no iteration over other projects).\n\n4. **Filter pushdown.** Status/label/projectID filters apply at the per-file / per-entry parse boundary, not post-materialization. Benchmark captures before/after for the common filtered case (e.g., `status=open`).\n\n5. **Perf targets on fixture (10 projects × 500 beads = 5000 beads total):**\n   - `bead(id:)` p95 ≤ 50ms in-process / ≤ 200ms HTTP (already covered by `ddx-ad0db8fd`; this bead may inherit the work).\n   - `beadsByProject(first=50)` p95 ≤ 75ms in-process / ≤ 250ms HTTP.\n   - `beads(first=50, no projectID)` p95 ≤ 200ms in-process / ≤ 500ms HTTP.\n   These are targets for the **first** pass (scope + pushdown). If they are not met with (1)+(2), escalate to the in-memory index (step 3 of Part 2 in the description) and update these targets with the new baseline.\n\n6. **Stale-read safety.** Regardless of fix chosen, a test writes a bead via `ddx bead update`, then queries it via GraphQL, then asserts the new value is visible on the very next request. Works under concurrency: N concurrent readers + writers interleaved without torn reads or panics.\n\n7. **CI posture (soft).** The harness is runnable via `make bench-graphql` (or the project's equivalent). CI wiring to fail on \u003e25% regression is a follow-up bead — recorded in this bead's notes with the proposed threshold but not implemented here.\n\n8. **Playwright smoke.** Opening `/beads` on the fixture project returns an interactive (clickable) list within 1s. Opening the cross-project `/beads` page returns within 2s. These are ceilings, not p95 targets — guards against outright regression, not fine-grained perf.\n\n9. **Cross-reference.** This bead's notes list `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail) and `ddx-2ceb02fa` (sessions shard/index) as the other two bead/session perf threads that should inherit this harness.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"7a2078a59d9885cb94f4c188ea95ce5149f969cd","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:41:52.271330549Z","description":"## Observed\n\nPer-project `/beads` page is noticeably slow; the cross-project \"all beads\" view is substantially slower. Related: `ddx-ad0db8fd` already identified the single-bead-detail slowness caused by the same storage pattern.\n\n## Root cause (same pattern, different call site)\n\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver_beads.go:22,40` —\n\n- `Beads` (cross-project) and `BeadsByProject` (single-project) both call `r.State.GetBeadSnapshots(status, label, projectID, search)`.\n- `GetBeadSnapshots` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:49-105`) iterates every registered project, constructs a `bead.Store` per project, calls `ReadAll()` (scan + JSONL parse of every bead file), filters in Go. Cost is `O(projects × beads_per_project)` on every call, independent of the filter/page size.\n- All filtering (status, label, project, search) is Go-side, post-read. The page-size window is applied in `beadConnectionFromSnapshots` after the full corpus is already materialized.\n- Cross-project \"all beads\" = same loop, just without the projectID filter. It is the worst case by design.\n\nThis is the same shape that's breaking the Sessions feed (`ddx-2ceb02fa`) and single-bead detail (`ddx-ad0db8fd`). A point fix here is insufficient — we need a measurement surface first so we stop relying on anecdote.\n\n## Proposed direction\n\n### Part 1 — Perf harness (foundational; enables everything else)\n\nIntroduce a reusable GraphQL perf harness under `cli/internal/server/perf/` or `cli/internal/server/graphql/bench_test.go`:\n\n- Seeded fixture builder: generates N projects × M beads with configurable distribution of status/labels/dependencies. Reuses existing store primitives (no fake data layer).\n- Go benchmark + scriptable HTTP runner (wrapper around an already-running server) reporting p50/p95/p99 per query shape.\n- Targets covered on day one: `bead(id:)`, `beadsByProject(projectID, first, after, status, label, search)`, `beads(projectID=nil, first, after, status, label)`, `docGraph`, `sessions` (so the sessions shard work `ddx-2ceb02fa` inherits this harness). New query targets are added by writing a single table row.\n- Thresholds are **recorded, not asserted**, in the first pass. CI surfaces a regression if a threshold is breached beyond a configured delta (e.g., +25% p95). Initial pass is \"land the harness and record baseline\"; a follow-up wires it into CI.\n\n### Part 2 — Fix the scan pattern\n\nMinimum viable, in priority order:\n\n1. **Scope by project when the query is scoped.** `BeadsByProject` should call a new `GetBeadSnapshotsForProject(projectID, filters…)` that only opens that one project's store. Trivial change; eliminates the cross-project factor for the common case.\n2. **Early filter pushdown.** `GetBeadSnapshots` should apply status/label/projectID filters at the file-parse boundary where cheap, not after materializing every bead. (Still reads every file — deeper fix below.)\n3. **Lightweight in-memory index with invalidation.** After (1) and (2), if \"all beads\" or search is still slow, introduce an index keyed `{projectID, beadID}` with basic metadata (status, labels, title, updatedAt). Refresh on bead write + debounced filesystem watch. Full-body reads stay lazy — the index never holds bodies. This is the same storage discipline we adopted for sessions in `ddx-2ceb02fa`.\n\nThe harness from Part 1 is the gate for choosing between (2) and (3); don't commit to the index without numbers.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Full-text search infrastructure (tracked separately if needed).\n- Changing the on-disk bead format.\n- Cross-node federation performance. Current concern is a single server reading a single workspace.","events_attachment":"ddx-9ce6842a/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"stalled: read_only_tools_exceeded","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"execute-loop-retry-after":"2026-04-23T10:04:40Z","feature":"FEAT-008","id":"ddx-9ce6842a","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-008","feat-004","perf","graphql"],"notes":"## 2026-04-22 — User directives on fixture size and storage strategy\n\nTwo standing instructions from the user that apply to this bead and to all other perf beads (`ddx-ad0db8fd`, `ddx-2ceb02fa`, and any spawned from efficacy/providers work):\n\n1. **Minimum fixture size: 1,000s of records.** The original acceptance criterion (10 projects × 500 beads = 5,000) already meets this for beads, but restate the floor explicitly: **no perf work lands against a fixture smaller than 1,000 records per query shape**. Sessions fixtures, efficacy fixtures, provider/quota history fixtures — all ≥1k rows. Beads fixture stays at 5k; sessions fixture for `ddx-2ceb02fa` should be ≥10k index entries (already specified); efficacy and any new surfaces ≥1k attempts/rows each.\n\n2. **DB backend is on the table.** If in-place optimization (scope, pushdown, lightweight index) cannot hit the target latencies on the fixture sizes above, the user is open to introducing a real data store — candidates named: **axon**, **sqlite**, **postgres**. Do not silently pick one: **raise the decision with the user before choosing a path.** Capture the baseline, capture what in-place optimization achieved, then bring the choice (and the rationale) to the user.\n\nThese directives supersede any acceptance criterion that implies \"optimize in place no matter what.\" If the numbers say storage needs to change, that's the outcome — but the user decides the substrate.\n\n**AC §8 — Playwright cross-project test routes wrong path**: `beads-smoke.spec.ts:107` navigates to `/nodes/${NODE_INFO.id}/projects/${PROJECT_ID}/beads` for the \"cross-project\" test, which is the same per-project URL as the 1s test. A cross-project smoke test should hit the all-beads route (e.g., `/nodes/${NODE_INFO.id}/beads` or whatever the actual cross-project URL is) and mock the `beads` query, not `beadsByProject`. The mock for `beads` exists (lines 76-89) but is never exercised because both tests navigate to the project-scoped path.\n**AC §9 — Bead notes cross-reference**: The acceptance criteria require this bead's notes to list `ddx-ad0db8fd` and `ddx-2ceb02fa`. The baseline `.md` and code comments mention them, but no `ddx bead update` call or notes update appears in the diff. If the notes were updated via a separate mechanism not captured in this branch, this finding can be dismissed.\n\n## 2026-04-23 — Baseline and perf-thread cross-reference\n\nBaseline recorded in `docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/2026-04-22-baseline.md` and JSON sibling from the 10 projects x 500 beads fixture (5,000 beads total, 1,000 session rows). Environment: `go=go1.26.2 goos=linux goarch=arm64 cpu-cores=12`.\n\nKey p95 results: `bead` 6.72ms in-process / 6.80ms HTTP; `beadsByProject` 8.84ms in-process / 9.29ms HTTP; `beads_crossProject` 62.16ms in-process / 75.80ms HTTP; `beadsByProject_statusOpen` 6.45ms in-process / 6.87ms HTTP.\n\nCross-reference for inherited harness work: `ddx-ad0db8fd` covers single-bead detail lookup; `ddx-2ceb02fa` covers sessions shard/index work. Both should use/extend this GraphQL perf harness.\n\nReview follow-up: AC §8 cross-project Playwright smoke now navigates to `/nodes/{nodeId}/beads` and exercises the `beads` GraphQL query instead of the project-scoped `beadsByProject` path. Proposed CI posture remains a follow-up +25% p95 regression gate, not implemented in this bead.\n\n## 2026-04-23 — Baseline refresh after session-index fixture alignment\n\nRe-ran `BENCH_REPORT_DATE=2026-04-22 make bench-graphql` after updating the perf fixture to seed current pointer-only session-index shards instead of legacy `sessions.jsonl`. Refreshed artifacts: `docs/helix/06-iterate/perf/2026-04-22-baseline.md` and `.json`.\n\nRefreshed p95 results: `bead` 7.23ms in-process / 7.29ms HTTP; `beadsByProject` 7.18ms in-process / 7.83ms HTTP; `beads_crossProject` 62.49ms in-process / 67.29ms HTTP; `beadsByProject_statusOpen` 6.74ms in-process / 7.73ms HTTP; `agentSessions` 13.45ms in-process / 14.19ms HTTP.\n\n## 2026-04-23 — Final `make bench-graphql` verification\n\nAfter fixing `cli/Makefile` to pass an absolute `DDX_BENCH_REPORT_DIR`, `BENCH_REPORT_DATE=2026-04-22 make bench-graphql` writes to the intended repo docs path. Final p95 results from that run: `bead` 6.76ms in-process / 7.20ms HTTP; `beadsByProject` 6.88ms in-process / 7.85ms HTTP; `beads_crossProject` 63.22ms in-process / 71.82ms HTTP; `beadsByProject_statusOpen` 6.75ms in-process / 7.56ms HTTP; `agentSessions` 13.24ms in-process / 13.64ms HTTP.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\nReview payload is insufficient: the only commit shown is the execution-evidence chore. Re-run the review against the full branch diff (commits `9dc0a408` and any predecessors introducing the harness, `BeadsByProject` scope fix, filter pushdown, `make bench-graphql`, baseline markdown, and Playwright specs) so each AC can be graded against real code.\nExecution events in `manifest.json` also record a prior iter-1 finding (Playwright cross-project test in `beads-smoke.spec.ts:107` navigated to the per-project URL rather than the all-beads route) and a notes-cross-reference gap; confirm both are resolved in the full diff before approving AC §8 and §9.","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-ace9ade3","status":"closed","title":"Bead list and cross-project all-beads are too slow: establish a GraphQL perf harness and fix N·M scan pattern","updated_at":"2026-04-25T07:36:55.005145669Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:538:{"acceptance":"1. push_failed flow attempts auto pull --rebase / merge / retry-push at least once before exiting with push_failed. 2. push_failed cooldown caps at 24h regardless of underlying error. 3. push_failed-with-conflicts (auto-merge fails) emits a structured outcome distinct from generic execution_failed; bead is parked for human review with the conflict context recorded as a structured event (kind:push-conflict). 4. Regression test: a fake git that returns non-fast-forward on first push, then fast-forward after pull/merge, exercises the new flow and asserts the bead lands cleanly without operator intervention. 5. Regression test: a fake git that produces an unresolvable merge conflict exercises the structured-outcome path and asserts cooldown \u003c= 24h. 6. CHANGELOG entry. 7. Update ddx-fba752b9's CHANGELOG section (or a new section) noting that the 24h cap applies to all loop-set cooldowns, not just push failures.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-29T06:55:32Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"131811","closing_commit_sha":"ae1ab304e7da3abdae214b2fe3abd0e95788b4ac","created_at":"2026-04-29T03:45:33.85811609Z","description":"Today, when execute-bead lands locally but push fails (e.g. non-fast-forward because remote advanced), the loop sets execute-loop-retry-after to ONE YEAR in the future. This is wrong on multiple axes:\n\n1. Push race is fundamentally recoverable: pull/merge/push is the standard fix and takes seconds.\n2. A 1-year cooldown means the bead never re-runs in any reasonable working horizon.\n3. The work is already done locally — the bead is functionally complete; reopening it for a year is misleading.\n\nObserved in this session on ddx-98e6e9ef (routing-preflight-gate): worker landed commit 978131c6, push rejected because origin/main had advanced (687fb4ca + 4a6c6e8a pushed remotely between the local land and the push). Loop set retry_after=2027-04-29T03:43:59Z. Operator had to manually merge, push, and clear the cooldown.\n\nWhat is needed:\n\n1. push_failed should auto-pull/merge/retry-push at least once before giving up. The merge case where origin advanced with non-overlapping work is the common case in a multi-worker / multi-operator setup.\n\n2. If the auto-merge has conflicts that the loop cannot resolve, push_failed should set a SHORT cooldown (5-15 min), not 1 year. The operator just needs time to resolve the conflict.\n\n3. push_failed-with-conflicts should mark the bead as needing-human-review (a structured outcome similar to declined_needs_decomposition from ddx-fba752b9), not as a generic execution failure.\n\n4. The loop's cooldown duration should NEVER exceed some reasonable cap (e.g. 24h) for any outcome. Year-scale cooldowns mean 'never' and that should be a deliberate operator choice via 'ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...', not an automatic loop decision.","events_attachment":"ddx-a458af7c/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-29T07:17:02.559534515Z","id":"ddx-a458af7c","issue_type":"bug","labels":["execute-loop","beads","quality-of-life"],"owner":"ddx","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-d220ff36","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: push_failed should NOT set a 1-year cooldown — race-with-remote is recoverable","updated_at":"2026-04-29T07:17:59.668614078Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:566:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As a developer browsing the web UI beads view, I want clicking a bead to open its detail pane within ~200ms on a repo with hundreds of beads across several projects, so the UI feels interactive.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. A benchmark/harness exists (Go benchmark or scripted GraphQL client) that measures end-to-end `bead(id:)` latency against a fixture repo with ≥ 5 projects and ≥ 500 beads total. Output is a p50/p95 number, not a pass/fail bool.\n2. Baseline is recorded in the bead notes (actual number measured on the current `main` binary at the time of fix).\n3. After the fix, p95 for `bead(id:)` on the fixture is ≤ 50ms in-process / ≤ 200ms over HTTP on a dev laptop.\n4. The fix does not introduce a stale-read regression: writing a bead via `ddx bead update` and then fetching it via GraphQL returns the new value on the next request (verified by a test that does write-then-read).\n5. Concurrency-safe: an integration test spawns N concurrent `bead(id:)` queries interleaved with `bead update` mutations and asserts no panics, no torn reads, and consistent responses.\n6. Playwright e2e: open `/nodes/.../beads`, click a bead row, assert the sidepane shows the bead title within 1s (generous budget; guards against regressions not the p95).","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"c1822352b4bbfa4181f96a203bce522d249f342d","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:07:18.806879855Z","description":"## Observed\n\nOpening a bead in the sidepane on `/nodes/.../projects/.../beads?q=test` takes 5+ seconds. The beads list renders quickly; the delay is on single-bead lookup.\n\n## Root cause\n\n`cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver.go:93-100` — the `Bead(id)` resolver calls `r.State.GetBeadSnapshots(\"\", \"\", \"\", \"\")` with no filters, which at `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:49-105` walks **every project**, constructs a `bead.Store` per project, calls `ReadAll()` (full directory scan + JSONL parse of every bead file), then iterates the result in Go to find one matching ID.\n\nSo every sidepane open = `O(projects × beads_per_project)` disk reads + parses, for a lookup that should be `O(1)` or `O(log n)`.\n\n`bead.Store` already exposes `Get(id)` (`cli/internal/bead/store.go:450`), but the resolver doesn't use it — nor does it know which project the bead lives in, because the GraphQL schema exposes `bead(id:)` without a project scope.\n\n## Proposed direction (not prescriptive)\n\nAny of these would resolve it; pick based on measurements:\n\n1. **Project scope the query.** Change schema to `bead(projectId:, id:)` (or accept a composite global id) and call `Store.Get` directly.\n2. **Maintain an in-memory index** `beadID → projectID` refreshed on bead mutations and on a debounced filesystem watcher; resolver does one map lookup + one `Store.Get`.\n3. **Short-TTL snapshot cache** behind `GetBeadSnapshots`, invalidated on bead writes.\n\nBefore picking: add a baseline measurement harness (see acceptance) so we know the actual p50/p95 before and after, not just anecdote.","events_attachment":"ddx-ad0db8fd/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model \"minimax/minimax-m2.7\" for harness \"gemini\"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"feature":"FEAT-008","id":"ddx-ad0db8fd","issue_type":"bug","labels":["feat-008","feat-004","perf","graphql"],"notes":"Baseline/after measurement for bead(id:) lookup fixture (5 projects, 500 total beads, go test ./internal/server -run ^$ -bench \"BenchmarkGraphQLBeadLookup(LegacyScan)?Latency$\" -benchtime=80x -count=1, linux/arm64):\n\n- Legacy full-scan path matching main resolver behavior before fix: p50=13.38ms, p95=14.81ms.\n- Optimized resolver path after bead ID -\u003e project index warmup from beadsByProject: p50=2.593ms, p95=3.156ms.\n\nFocused latency tests on the same fixture also measured GetBeadSnapshot p95=3.105871ms and GraphQL bead(id:) p95=3.450579ms over 80 requests.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\nMissing implementation change in `cli/internal/server/graphql/resolver.go`: `Bead(id:)` is not shown using a scoped lookup, cache, or index instead of scanning all bead snapshots.\nMissing benchmark or scripted GraphQL latency harness for a fixture repo with at least 5 projects and 500 beads.\nMissing bead-note update recording the baseline p50/p95 measured on the current `main` binary.\nMissing post-fix performance evidence showing p95 within the required in-process or HTTP budget.\nMissing write-then-read regression test covering `ddx bead update` followed by GraphQL `bead(id:)`.\nMissing concurrent integration test interleaving `bead(id:)` queries with bead updates.\nMissing Playwright e2e test for opening `/nodes/.../beads`, clicking a bead row, and rendering the sidepane title within 1s.","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-2d232da6","status":"closed","title":"Bead detail GraphQL resolver scans every project and every bead on each request","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:26:31.775822002Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:575:{"acceptance":"AC1. `ddx agent route-status reset` (or equivalent verb) exists and clears the trailing-window adaptive metric for the current project, returning min-tier evaluation to a clean baseline. Output names every file/store touched.\n\nAC2. `ddx agent doctor` (or `ddx agent route-status`) prints current adaptive state per tier: window size, trailing attempts, success rate, threshold, current effective floor, and whether the tier is currently being skipped. Diagnostic output is enough to debug a 'why is cheap being skipped' scenario without reading source.\n\nAC3. Routing failures classified as 'no viable provider', 'harness not installed', or 'configuration error' are recorded but EXCLUDED from the adaptive success-rate computation — only failures that reached an actual model invocation count.\n\nAC4. Documentation in docs/ describes the adaptive min-tier mechanism, the metric store path and schema, the reset workflow, and the relationship between --no-adaptive-min-tier (temporary bypass) and reset (permanent state clear).\n\nAC5. After running the reset CLI, a fresh `execute-loop --once --local` evaluates cheap tier on its own merits without the prior trailing-window verdict carrying over.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","created_at":"2026-04-29T03:32:12.543616417Z","description":"execute-loop's adaptive min-tier promotion (escalation.AdaptiveMinTierThreshold etc.) silently skips cheap tier when trailing cheap-tier success rate falls under threshold. Today the only visible signal is a single log line:\n\n  adaptive min-tier: skipping cheap tier (trailing success rate 0.00 over 9 attempts; threshold 0.20) — min-tier=standard\n\nThere is no documented or discoverable way to reset this state. Issues with the current design:\n\n1. STATE LOCATION IS NOT DOCUMENTED. The trailing-window metric is computed from .ddx/agent-logs/routing-outcomes.jsonl per-project (and possibly other sources — rotating that file alone did NOT clear the 0% verdict in a recent test, so additional state may live elsewhere). A user who wants to reset cannot find authoritative documentation of where the state lives.\n\n2. NO RESET CLI. There is no `ddx agent route-status reset`, no `ddx agent metrics clear`, no `ddx agent doctor --reset-adaptive`. The only path is to edit/truncate JSONL files by hand, which is fragile and breaks compatibility with any future schema.\n\n3. SILENT NOISE. Every cheap-tier failure permanently affects future routing without surfacing to the user. If a configuration bug (e.g. the model_overrides issue tracked as ddx-5538aa5b) caused 9 immediate failures, the adaptive logic now blocks cheap tier even after the configuration is fixed. The user has no way to know 'this is stale state, not real performance signal' without reading source.\n\n4. MITIGATION VIA --no-adaptive-min-tier IS NOT A FIX, IT'S AN OVERRIDE. The flag bypasses the gating but also defeats the legitimate purpose of the metric. We need ability to RESET the metric and re-evaluate from a clean slate, not permanently disable the feature.\n\n5. THE THRESHOLD/WINDOW DEFAULTS ARE AGGRESSIVE. Trailing window of 9-50 attempts at threshold 0.20 means a single cheap-tier provider misconfiguration (which is easy to hit, see ddx-5538aa5b) condemns cheap-tier for many subsequent invocations. Meanwhile EVERY configuration error is recorded as cheap-tier failure even when the failure was 'no viable provider' rather than 'model produced bad output'.\n\nRequested behavior:\n\n- A first-class CLI: `ddx agent route-status reset [--scope cheap|all]` that clears the adaptive metric for the current project, with a confirm prompt by default.\n- A flag on `ddx agent doctor` that displays current adaptive state per tier (success rate, attempt count, threshold, current floor) so users can SEE the problem.\n- An option to record only EXECUTION outcomes (model produced output) in adaptive metrics, NOT no-viable-provider/configuration failures — those should be tracked separately and never count against a tier's reputation.\n- Documented location and schema for the metric store, so users who want to inspect or surgically edit understand the on-disk format.","events_attachment":"ddx-aec9d68c/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-aec9d68c","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:agent","area:routing","kind:design-flaw","ux"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK\n\nDiff contains only an execution result.json artifact; no CLI, doctor diagnostics, classification logic, or docs are present to evaluate against AC1–AC5.","priority":1,"status":"closed","title":"adaptive min-tier: provide a trivial reset; current state is sticky and unresettable","updated_at":"2026-04-29T18:42:36.467762418Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:595:{"acceptance":"1. New traces from a `ddx agent` invocation show `latency_ms != elapsed_ms` for events past turn 1.\n2. Sum of `latency_ms` across an entire session is \u003c= `elapsed_ms` of the last event.\n3. Existing trace files: documented as legacy in CHANGELOG; analysis tools may need to ignore `latency_ms` for runs older than the fix commit.\n4. Unit test added that simulates two consecutive llm.response events and asserts latency_ms reflects per-call duration, not cumulative.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-29T03:11:55Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"131811","closing_commit_sha":"2dcecec07fca649ffbcaa189350e0530ad00f540","created_at":"2026-04-29T00:11:34.159189978Z","description":"## Symptom\n\nEvery `llm.response` event in `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-claude-*.jsonl` has `latency_ms == elapsed_ms`. Examples from agent-claude-1776370404950042448.jsonl:\n\n```jsonl\n{\"data\":{\"elapsed_ms\":7373, \"latency_ms\":7373, \"turn\":1, ...}}\n{\"data\":{\"elapsed_ms\":7995, \"latency_ms\":7995, \"turn\":2, ...}}\n{\"data\":{\"elapsed_ms\":12054,\"latency_ms\":12054,\"turn\":3, ...}}\n```\n\n`elapsed_ms` is monotonic across the session (cumulative wall time), so per-call LLM latency is unrecoverable from the log.\n\n## Impact\n\n- Cannot answer \"what was the LLM-side latency for turn N?\" — required for any performance investigation.\n- Cannot diff the agent's local-LLM benchmark numbers against captured traces.\n- The lucebox-hub/dflash bench_server.py harness needs real per-call latency to validate against reality.\n\n## Likely root cause\n\nLogging code in `cli/internal/agent/session_log_format.go` (or equivalent emit site) is writing the wrong value into `latency_ms`. Probably copy-paste from the elapsed-time field. Per-call latency should be `t_response_received - t_request_sent` for that single call.\n\n## Fix sketch\n\nCapture the per-call latency inside the harness adapter (point where the LLM HTTP call is made) and pass it through to the event emitter. Add a unit test asserting `latency_ms \u003c elapsed_ms` for any non-first event.","events_attachment":"ddx-b3b9501a/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-29T03:16:25.341369101Z","id":"ddx-b3b9501a","issue_type":"bug","labels":["kind:bug","area:agent","area:measurement"],"owner":"ddx","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-2ff8d561","status":"closed","title":"BUG: latency_ms field in agent-claude-*.jsonl traces equals cumulative elapsed_ms","updated_at":"2026-04-29T03:17:04.032019102Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:607:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator on any page in the project, I always see whether a drain worker is running and how much work is queued. One click takes me to the worker overview where I can scale capacity up or down with a single button, without picking a harness or profile every time.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Nav indicator visible everywhere.**\n   - A component in `NavShell.svelte` renders the drain indicator on every route inside a selected project.\n   - Idle state: dot + \"Queue: X ready\".\n   - Active state: spinner + \"N workers · X ready\".\n   - Clicking navigates to the workers overview.\n   - Playwright: navigate to beads, documents, sessions, efficacy — assert indicator is present on each with the expected text.\n\n2. **Live updates.**\n   - The indicator updates within 2s when a worker starts, finishes, or changes state. Backed by the existing worker-progress subscription.\n   - When ready-bead count changes (a bead is created/closed), the indicator updates within 5s. Either subscription-driven or short poll.\n\n3. **Summary query.**\n   - New `queueAndWorkersSummary(projectId) { readyBeads runningWorkers totalWorkers }`. Returns zeros on unknown or empty projects — no error.\n   - Resolver is cheap: reads the ready count from the same path bead-status queries use, not by iterating every bead.\n\n4. **Overview page — count control.**\n   - Header shows \"Drain workers: N\" prominently.\n   - `+ Add worker` button dispatches a default-spec worker (`workerDispatch(kind: \"execute-loop\", args: null)`). On success the row appears in the table within 2s and the indicator updates.\n   - `− Remove worker` button stops the oldest-running drain worker. Confirm-gated (\"Stop worker-XYZ?\"). After confirm, the worker transitions to `stopped` within 2s.\n   - Help text below the buttons: \"Adds a general-purpose drain worker. Use the per-harness picker below for custom specs.\"\n\n5. **Default-spec + safety rail.**\n   - `.ddx/config.yaml: workers.default_spec` is respected by `+ Add worker`. Integration test asserts a configured `profile: cheap` is propagated.\n   - `.ddx/config.yaml: workers.max_count`: when set, `+ Add worker` refuses past the cap; button disabled with tooltip \"at workers.max_count limit\". Not set → no cap. Integration test covers both.\n\n6. **Playwright — flow.**\n   - Seeds a project with 3 ready beads, zero workers.\n   - Asserts indicator shows \"Queue: 3 ready\", dot state.\n   - Clicks indicator → navigates to overview.\n   - Clicks `+ Add worker` → asserts new row and indicator transitions to spinner + \"1 worker · 3 ready\".\n   - Clicks `+` again → asserts \"2 workers · 3 ready\".\n   - Clicks `− Remove worker` → asserts one worker stops and indicator shows \"1 worker · ...\" within 2s.\n   - Navigates to /beads → asserts indicator persists and stays live.\n\n7. **No regressions.**\n   - Existing Drain button on project home continues to work (dispatches the same kind). Can stay or be removed depending on design note; recommend keeping it as the initial-kickoff affordance when zero workers run.\n   - Existing Workers table rendering + live-phase subscription is unchanged.\n   - Existing per-worker Start/Stop from `ddx-69789664` coexists unchanged.\n\n8. **Cross-references.**\n   - Hard dependency: `ddx-05b4cc9d` (fixes `workersByProject` filter — without it, the running-workers count is always 0 on a per-project query).\n   - Adjacent: `ddx-69789664` (per-worker lifecycle + Sessions IA). These beads are complementary; implementation should not re-introduce either's functionality, just use it.\n   - Adjacent: `ddx-9ce6842a` (perf harness). The `queueAndWorkersSummary` resolver gets a perf target in that harness: p95 ≤ 30ms in-process on a 5k-bead fixture. No full scan allowed.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-25T09:52:17Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"196235","closing_commit_sha":"dbc9c373185249904bec9cd32e0409c7cb9743c0","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:03:03.880032436Z","description":"## Observed / Motivation\n\nToday the queue-drain worker is invisible unless the operator is on the project home page or the Workers tab. An operator on any other page (Beads, Documents, Efficacy) has no idea whether a drain is active, how many ready beads are left, or whether they should kick off more capacity. The per-project home page's \"Drain queue\" button is one-shot and offers no scaling control — you start one worker and that's it.\n\nUser feedback: the drain should be a **top-level always-visible affordance** with:\n1. A persistent spinner/badge in the global nav showing drain state + an at-a-glance count (ready beads, running workers).\n2. Clicking the badge goes to a workers overview where the operator can see what each worker is doing.\n3. That overview needs a \"number of workers\" control — `Add worker` / `Remove worker` to scale drain capacity up or down, not just start-one-at-a-time.\n\n## Why this is a new surface, not just a tweak to existing Workers page\n\n- **Global spinner** is a nav-shell concern, not a page concern. It needs to be mounted in `NavShell.svelte` so every route sees it.\n- **Worker-count control** is a different interaction model from the per-worker Start/Stop flow proposed in `ddx-69789664`. That flow is \"pick a harness + effort + filter, start one worker with those specs.\" This flow is \"I want N general-purpose drain workers; add/remove one at a time.\" Both should coexist.\n- A **worker-count affordance** implies a default worker spec (default harness, default profile). That's fine — it mirrors `ddx work` with no flags — but it has to be explicit so the operator knows what they're spawning.\n\n## Scope\n\n### Part 1 — Global drain indicator in `NavShell`\n\n- New element in the top nav bar, visible on every route (fits next to the theme toggle).\n- Two states:\n  - **Idle** (zero running drain workers): subtle dot + \"Queue: X ready\" (where X is project-scoped ready-bead count). Clicking navigates to the workers view.\n  - **Active** (≥1 running drain worker): animated spinner + \"N workers · X ready\". Clicking navigates to the workers view.\n- Data: new lightweight query `queueAndWorkersSummary(projectId) { readyBeads, runningWorkers, totalWorkers }`, subscribes to worker-progress events to stay fresh.\n- Scope: tied to the currently-selected project in the `ProjectPicker`. If no project is selected, the indicator is hidden.\n- Perf: the query must be cheap — must not trigger the N·M scan already tracked in `ddx-9ce6842a`. Depends on those fixes for the ready-bead count.\n\n### Part 2 — Workers overview with count control\n\n- Repurpose the existing `/workers` route (or add a separate `/workers/overview` — design call) to include a header section with:\n  - Large \"Drain workers: N\" number.\n  - `+` button (\"Add worker\") — dispatches a new default-spec drain worker via the existing `workerDispatch(kind: \"execute-loop\")` mutation.\n  - `−` button (\"Remove worker\") — stops the oldest-running drain worker. Confirm-gated.\n  - Small help text: \"Adds a general-purpose queue-drain worker. Use the per-harness picker below for custom specs.\"\n- The existing per-worker table and per-row Start/Stop (from `ddx-69789664`) remain for fine control.\n- \"Default spec\" is: harness unset (route via profile), profile `default`, no effort override. Operator can change the project default in config.\n\n### Part 3 — Defaults + config\n\n- `.ddx/config.yaml: workers.default_spec` — object with optional harness, profile, effort, min_tier, max_tier. Used by Part 2's `+ Add worker`. Unset = use `ddx work` defaults.\n- `.ddx/config.yaml: workers.max_count` — optional safety rail, default unset. When set, `+ Add worker` refuses past the cap and tooltips \"at workers.max_count limit\".\n\n### Part 4 — Empty/error states\n\n- Indicator on a project with no ready beads and zero workers: \"Queue: 0 ready — nothing to drain\". Clicking still navigates (so operator can spawn one anyway).\n- Indicator when the per-project workers query is broken (pre-`ddx-05b4cc9d`): falls back to the global count; logs a one-time console warning.\n- Add-worker failure: surfaces inline on the overview page (error toast + detail), doesn't silently drop.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Per-harness-specific queue views (e.g., \"drain with codex\" as a distinct indicator).\n- Bead prioritization rules for multi-worker draining — beads already have a priority field, and `execute-loop` already claims in ready-order. No change.\n- Concurrency safety review of multi-worker draining. It already works (beads are claimed atomically), but if `ddx-05b4cc9d` surfaces issues there, file a follow-up.","events_attachment":"ddx-b6cf025c/events.jsonl","execute-loop-heartbeat-at":"2026-04-25T09:59:17.30531356Z","feature":"FEAT-008","id":"ddx-b6cf025c","issue_type":"task","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","feat-006","ui","operator-ux"],"notes":"**`+layout.svelte` (workers overview)**: The `+ Add worker` button has no awareness of `workers.max_count`. The AC requires the button to be **disabled with a tooltip** (\"at workers.max_count limit\") when the cap is reached, rather than allowing the click and surfacing a server error. This needs the `queueAndWorkersSummary` query (or a new field) to expose the cap, and the button's `disabled` binding to incorporate it.\n**`workers.spec.ts`**: The e2e test asserts `dispatchCalled` and `stopCalled` booleans but does not verify the **indicator text transitions** after add/remove. AC #6 explicitly requires asserting \"1 worker · 3 ready\" after the first add, \"2 workers · 3 ready\" after the second add, and updated text after remove. The test also only clicks `+` once, not twice as the AC specifies. The mock's `QueueAndWorkersSummary` handler dynamically computes `runningWorkers` from the mocked `workers` array, so the data is available — the assertions are just missing.\n\n**`DrainIndicator.svelte:75-85`** — poll-only refresh via 3s `setInterval`. AC #2 requires subscription-driven updates (`subscribeWorkerProgress`) so worker-state changes reflect within 2s. Add a `workerProgress` subscription (filtered to the current project) that triggers `refresh(projectId)` on every event, keeping the 3s poll only as a backstop for ready-bead count changes.","owner":"ddx","priority":2,"session_id":"eb-46c62442","status":"closed","title":"Persistent drain-queue worker indicator in nav + N-worker count control on dashboard","updated_at":"2026-04-25T10:00:25.023687359Z"}
.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:627:{"acceptance":"1. testdata/omlx-wire/ contains at least 2 JSONL fixtures: one happy-path (successful bead close), one error-path (model-side failure cleanly recorded).\n\n2. cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go runs a ddx work execute-bead orchestration against a test-double provider that replays the fixtures, asserting: no 'unexpected end of JSON input', final outcome matches fixture expectation, bead state mutates correctly.\n\n3. Test runs in CI without requiring a live omlx endpoint.\n\n4. Opt-in live smoke test (//go:build omlx_smoke) covers the same happy-path scenario against a real vidar-omlx when invoked manually.\n\n5. README or docs/ entry describes how to regenerate fixtures via scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh when omlx server behavior changes.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-18T04:14:07Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"1833233","closing_commit_sha":"ad14ee84ff0faa5b8754f5274ea795fb1511cde7","created_at":"2026-04-18T03:01:36.207143767Z","description":"## Context\n\nddx-agent v0.3.14 shipped the SSE comment-frame filter that unblocks omlx. Manual dogfooding (2026-04-18) confirmed a single bead executing via vidar-omlx for 6+ minutes with 17,000+ streaming events before the fix caused the regression this bead is meant to prevent: we have no automated assertion that the omlx path actually stays healthy.\n\nBoth fresh-eyes reviews (codex + opus, 2026-04-18) flagged: 'missing bead for v0.3.14 consumer-side smoke test in DDx'. This is that bead.\n\n## Fix\n\nAdd an end-to-end smoke test that runs a real ddx work invocation against a recorded-or-live omlx endpoint and asserts the attempt closes (or at least produces a closing-status result: success / already_satisfied / a genuine model-side error) without surfacing 'unexpected end of JSON input' or silent stream termination.\n\nTwo implementation options:\n\n1. **Record + replay via virtual harness** (preferred for CI isolation). Capture a full AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1 trace of a successful vidar-omlx run, store it as a fixture, replay via a test double that emulates omlx's SSE quirks (keep-alive comment frames, reasoning warmup silence, reasoning_content deltas). Test asserts ddx-agent SDK consumes the full stream, ddx work's execute-bead orchestrator records closing status, and bead state mutates correctly.\n\n2. **Live smoke, opt-in** (developer-machine only). A test file guarded by build tag  that makes a real request to vidar-omlx. Useful for catching provider-side regressions (e.g., omlx server ships a breaking change). Skip in CI.\n\nDo BOTH: option 1 for CI signal, option 2 for manual release verification.\n\n## What this prevents\n\n- Regression: a future ddx-agent bump reintroduces the SSE-comment-frame bug or similar streaming defect. CI fixture test catches it before the bump merges.\n- Regression: DDx-side change breaks the omlx dispatch path (e.g., adds a header that trips omlx's parser). CI catches it.\n- Silent-success: ddx work completes but actually landed a bogus result_rev because of a subtle SSE-frame-dropping bug. Four shipped profile ladders: default=[cheap,standard,smart], cheap=[cheap], fast=[fast,smart], smart=[smart]. Documented in FEAT-006.\n\n3. Tier escalation in routing.go / execute_bead_orchestrator.go iterates profile_ladders[P] on failure, respecting --provider affinity (ddx-3c5ba7cc) and capability gating (ddx-4817edfd). Escalation triggers: execution_failed, land_conflict, post_run_check_failed, structural_validation_failed. no_changes uses the existing cooldown path.\n\n4. Bead routing event records: requested_profile, requested_tier, resolved_tier, escalation_count, final_tier. A run that escalates cheap -\u003e standard -\u003e success at standard has an event with escalation_count=1, resolved_tier=standard, final_tier=standard.\n\n5. 'ddx work --profile default' drains the queue by trying local first and escalating only when needed. Measured outcome over a test run of 10 representative beads shows at least 50% closing at the cheap tier (target: most work lands locally; escalations are the exception).\n\n6. Regression tests cover each ladder path: success-at-cheap, escalate-cheap-to-standard, escalate-all-tiers-then-exhaustion, --max-tier caps the ladder, --profile cheap never escalates.\n\n7. FEAT-006 Profile Routing section updated with the ladder semantics and the intent table (default/cheap/fast/smart).","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"93f938c41c93be78fc199bd150f455add6575d24","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:51:49.512908486Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-bbb65768","depends_on_id":"ddx-2d974641","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-18T02:52:01Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-bbb65768","depends_on_id":"ddx-e7254128","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T13:58:09Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-bbb65768","depends_on_id":"ddx-7bc0c8d5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-20T14:36:21Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-bbb65768","depends_on_id":"ddx-ea7f041c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:36:16Z"}],"description":"## Target UX\n\nThe user-facing profile vocabulary (cheap / default / fast / smart) should map to concrete routing policies that take full advantage of local models without blocking on their limits. Specifically:\n\n### default profile (the common case)\nTry local models first, escalate to cloud if:\n- local model's context window is too small for the bead prompt\n- local model lacks a capability the bead requires (tools, structured output, etc.)\n- local dispatch fails (provider unreachable, timeout, model-side error)\n- bead reopens due to post-run check failure (review-malfunction, land-conflict, etc.)\n\nEscalation target: next tier up in the ladder (standard cloud — e.g. codex gpt-5.4 within subscription). If THAT fails, escalate once more to smart cloud. Then stop and unclaim.\n\n### cheap profile\nLocal only. Never escalate. Intended for high-volume exploratory work where cost dominates and adequate-quality is sufficient. If the cheap tier can't serve the bead, the bead unclaims and waits.\n\n### fast profile\nCloud-first, skip local entirely. Intended for latency-critical or interactive work where local inference's 9s warmup is unacceptable. Starts at a fast cloud model (e.g. gemini-flash or haiku) and escalates to smart on failure.\n\n### smart profile\nCloud-high-quality from the start. Intended for bead-review, adversarial checks, or beads whose AC demands high-quality reasoning. No escalation — if smart fails, the work fails meaningfully.\n\n## Why this matters (dogfooding signal)\n\nAs of 2026-04-18, DDx's default is cheap = qwen/qwen3.6 local (vidar-omlx). With v0.3.14 shipping the SSE-comment-frame filter, the local path actually works end-to-end. The queue can drain mostly on local hardware for pennies per bead, escalating only when the bead genuinely needs cloud quality. That's the throughput-per-dollar goal (goal:throughput-per-dollar label).\n\n## Prereqs (already filed under ddx-2d974641 or sibling beads)\n\nThis epic is blocked on the autorouting epic delivering its exit-gate UX — 'declare provider + use canonical ref + autorouting works'. Specifically:\n\n- ddx-8610020e (RouteRequest.Provider field) — foundation\n- ddx-0486e601 (fuzzy normalization + per-provider) — lets cheap-tier model refs resolve to provider-native IDs\n- ddx-2f5a2284 (catalog/Profile discovery integration) — lets Profile tier overrides retarget to provider-native IDs\n- ddx-3c5ba7cc (tier escalation --provider affinity) — the lower half of this epic's 'escalate on failure' semantics\n- ddx-4817edfd (capability gating) — prevents routing to a model that can't serve the bead\n\nWith those in place, the profile-level policy here is mostly CONFIGURATION + a small amount of scoring-layer logic.\n\n## Scope\n\n### Config schema additions\n\n.ddx/config.yaml agent.routing extends with an explicit per-profile ladder:\n\n    agent:\n      routing:\n        profile_ladders:\n          default: [cheap, standard, smart]      # local first, escalate\n          cheap:   [cheap]                        # local only\n          fast:    [fast, smart]                  # cloud fast, escalate to smart\n          smart:   [smart]                        # cloud high-quality, no escalation\n        model_overrides:\n          cheap:    qwen/qwen3.6\n          fast:     kimi/k2.5\n          standard: codex/gpt-5.4                # new: subscription cloud, mid-quality\n          smart:    minimax/minimax-m2.7\n\nCurrent profile_priority field (a flat list) becomes a legacy alias for profile_ladders.default with a deprecation warning.\n\n### Tier-escalation policy (routing.go)\n\nFor a given profile P, iterate profile_ladders[P]:\n- Evaluate model_overrides[tier] resolves on a viable candidate (with --provider affinity from ddx-3c5ba7cc).\n- If yes, try it; on failure (execution_failed / land_conflict / post_run_check_failed), move to next tier.\n- Record tier decision + escalation chain in routing event on the bead so telemetry reflects where work actually landed.\n\n### User-facing CLI\n\n- ddx work --profile default — the new primary invocation. Most users run this.\n- ddx work --profile cheap / fast / smart — opt-in deviations.\n- --max-tier continues to cap the ladder (explicit user override beats config).\n\n### Telemetry + measurement\n\nEvery execution event records: requested_profile, requested_tier, resolved_tier, escalation_count, final_tier. Aggregated metrics (FEAT-016 / ddx-4b59b816) report per-profile throughput, per-tier cost, per-bead escalation rate.\n\n## Exit gate\n\n'ddx work --profile default --once --local' against a ready bead on a host with a healthy local provider AND healthy cloud fallback:\n- Claims the bead.\n- Tries cheap tier (local). If it succeeds, closes bead with tier=cheap recorded.\n- If cheap fails for any escalation-triggering reason, automatically retries at standard tier without operator intervention.\n- If standard fails, retries at smart.\n- Final outcome recorded faithfully (success at whatever tier landed, or exhaustion if all tiers failed).\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Dynamic profile selection ('auto-pick profile based on bead complexity'). Too much magic; explicit profile choice is fine.\n- Cost budgets / circuit breakers. FEAT-014 (token awareness) tracks this separately.\n- Per-bead profile overrides (bead-level 'requires-profile: smart'). Could be a future follow-up but out of scope here.\n\n## Metadata\n\nGoverning spec: FEAT-006 (agent service) + the autorouting epic ddx-2d974641. This bead is the PROFILE-layer policy that sits on top of the routing fixes from ddx-2d974641. Do not start until ddx-2d974641's Phase-1 children are closed.","events_attachment":"ddx-bbb65768/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-bbb65768","issue_type":"epic","labels":["ddx","phase:build","kind:design","area:agent","area:routing","area:profiles","goal:throughput-per-dollar","wave:deferred","workstream:agent-upgrade"],"priority":2,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"profile/automation: default = local-first with escalation; fast/smart = cloud-first","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:03:22.145529072Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:934:{"acceptance":"SD-015 defines a stable model-level attribution key for routing metrics so RoutingOutcome, QuotaSnapshot, and any derived burn/performance summaries can be associated with the resolved candidate model or canonical target; FEAT-014 stays consistent with that key; and the docs no longer imply that latency/cost/reliability from different models on the same surface are mixed into one routing signal","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","claimed-at":"2026-04-10T03:46:32Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"327749","closing_commit_sha":"0364b777bb79faf9eeef12a4d940b190d687c894","created_at":"2026-04-10T03:45:04.557331352Z","description":"Review finding from fresh-eyes review.\nFile: docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md:175\nCategory: correctness\nSeverity: medium\nDescription: The new minimal-routing schema records `RoutingOutcome`, `QuotaSnapshot`, and `BurnSummary` only by `harness` and `surface`. Earlier in the same design, however, each `CandidatePlan` carries `CanonicalTarget`, `ConcreteModel`, `EstimatedCostUSD`, and `PerformanceMetrics` because routing decisions can differ materially by resolved model or exact pin. A single surface can host very different models with different latency, reliability, and cost characteristics. If DDx aggregates outcome and burn data only at the harness/surface level, runs for one model will contaminate routing signals for another, making model-specific scoring and exact-pin behavior incorrect.\nSuggested fix: Extend the minimal routing-metrics schema with a model-identifying dimension such as `canonical_target` or `concrete_model` (or explicitly define an equivalent normalization key), and update FEAT-014/SD-015 so performance, cost, and burn inputs remain attributable to the resolved model rather than only the surface.","id":"hx-4a12a619","issue_type":"task","labels":["helix","phase:build","review-finding","severity:medium","area:agent","area:docs"],"owner":"erik","priority":0,"spec-id":"docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md","status":"closed","title":"correctness: keep routing metrics model-specific","updated_at":"2026-04-10T03:47:24.607997607Z"}

The first\\n+// line is the {\\\"kind\\\":\\\"meta\\\", ...} record; remaining {\\\"kind\\\":\\\"frame\\\",\\\"body\\\":...}\\n+// lines are the SSE bodies to stream in order.\\n+func loadOMLXFixture(t *testing.T, path string) omlxFixture {\\n+\\tt.Helper()\\n+\\tf, err := os.Open(path) // #nosec G304 -- test fixtures are checked in.\\n+\\trequire.NoError(t, err, \\\"open fixture %s\\\", path)\\n+\\tt.Cleanup(func() { _ = f.Close() })\\n+\\n+\\tvar fix omlxFixture\\n+\\tscanner := bufio.NewScanner(f)\\n+\\t// Default 64KB scanner buffer is tight for long reasoning lines.\\n+\\tscanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 64*1024), 1024*1024)\\n+\\tfirst := true\\n+\\tfor scanner.Scan() {\\n+\\t\\tline := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())\\n+\\t\\tif line == \\\"\\\" {\\n+\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n+\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\tvar record struct {\\n+\\t\\t\\tKind string          `json:\\\"kind\\\"`\\n+\\t\\t\\tBody string          `json:\\\"body\\\"`\\n+\\t\\t\\tRaw  json.RawMessage `json:\\\"-\\\"`\\n+\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); err != nil {\\n+\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"parsing fixture line in %s: %v\\\", path, err)\\n+\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\tswitch record.Kind {\\n+\\t\\tcase \\\"meta\\\":\\n+\\t\\t\\tif !first {\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"fixture %s: meta record must be the first non-empty line\\\", path)\\n+\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\t\\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &fix.Meta); err != nil {\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"parsing meta in %s: %v\\\", path, err)\\n+\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\tcase \\\"frame\\\":\\n+\\t\\t\\tfix.Frames = append(fix.Frames, record.Body)\\n+\\t\\tdefault:\\n+\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"fixture %s: unknown record kind %q\\\", path, record.Kind)\\n+\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\tfirst = false\\n+\\t}\\n+\\trequire.NoError(t, scanner.Err(), \\\"scanning fixture %s\\\", path)\\n+\\trequire.NotEmpty(t, fix.Meta.Name, \\\"fixture %s: missing meta.name\\\", path)\\n+\\trequire.NotEmpty(t, fix.Frames, \\\"fixture %s: no frame records\\\", path)\\n+\\treturn fix\\n+}\\n+\\n+// newOMLXReplayServer stands up an httptest.Server that behaves like a vidar-omlx\\n+// /v1/chat/completions endpoint by replaying the fixture's frames verbatim,\\n+// with a short inter-frame flush so the consumer's SSE decoder sees per-chunk\\n+// arrivals rather than one batched blob.\\n+func newOMLXReplayServer(t *testing.T, frames []string) *httptest.Server {\\n+\\tt.Helper()\\n+\\tsrv := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n+\\t\\t// Match omlx's advertised Content-Type exactly — the SSE comment-frame\\n+\\t\\t// middleware in provider/openai activates only when Content-Type starts\\n+\\t\\t// with text/event-stream.\\n+\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"text/event-stream; charset=utf-8\\\")\\n+\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Cache-Control\\\", \\\"no-cache\\\")\\n+\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Transfer-Encoding\\\", \\\"chunked\\\")\\n+\\t\\tflusher, _ := w.(http.Flusher)\\n+\\t\\tfor _, body := range frames {\\n+\\t\\t\\tif _, err := io.WriteString(w, body); err != nil {\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n+\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\t\\tif flusher != nil {\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tflusher.Flush()\\n+\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\t\\t// Small delay to reproduce per-frame arrival; long enough that the\\n+\\t\\t\\t// decoder cannot batch frames, short enough that the test stays\\n+\\t\\t\\t// under a second total.\\n+\\t\\t\\ttime.Sleep(5 * time.Millisecond)\\n+\\t\\t}\\n+\\t}))\\n+\\tt.Cleanup(srv.Close)\\n+\\treturn srv\\n+}\\n+\\n+// TestOMLXFixtureReplay_ConsumerEndToEnd drives a full ddx work orchestration\\n+// path (Runner.RunAgent → agentlib.Run → openai.Provider.ChatStream) against an\\n+// httptest double that replays recorded omlx SSE wire traces. This is the\\n+// consumer-side smoke test for ddx-agent v0.3.14 — see bead ddx-bbb2d177.\\n+//\\n+// What this guards against:\\n+//\\n+//  1. A future ddx-agent bump reintroducing the SSE comment-frame bug (bead\\n+//     agent-f237e07b): the happy-path fixture includes `: keep-alive\\\\n\\\\n`\\n+//     frames between data frames and must NOT surface \\\"unexpected end of\\n+//     JSON input\\\".\\n+//  2. A DDx-side change breaking the omlx dispatch path (e.g., a header or\\n+//     request-shape change that trips omlx's parser).\\n+//  3. A silent-success regression where the stream is consumed without error\\n+//     but the assembled content is empty or malformed — the happy-path assertion\\n+//     pins both the final content and finish_reason.\\n+//  4. Error-path regressions where a real model-side failure is swallowed by\\n+//     the consumer — the error-path fixture emits a malformed data frame and\\n+//     asserts the consumer surfaces the error with a specific signature (and\\n+//     specifically NOT \\\"unexpected end of JSON input\\\", which would mean the\\n+//     SSE filter regressed).\\n+func TestOMLXFixtureReplay_ConsumerEndToEnd(t *testing.T) {\\n+\\tfixtureDir := filepath.Join(\\\"testdata\\\", \\\"omlx-wire\\\")\\n+\\tcases := []string{\\\"happy_path.jsonl\\\", \\\"error_path.jsonl\\\"}\\n+\\n+\\tfor _, name := range cases {\\n+\\t\\tname := name\\n+\\t\\tt.Run(name, func(t *testing.T) {\\n+\\t\\t\\tfix := loadOMLXFixture(t, filepath.Join(fixtureDir, name))\\n+\\n+\\t\\t\\tsrv := newOMLXReplayServer(t, fix.Frames)\\n+\\n+\\t\\t\\tprovider := openai.New(openai.Config{\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tBaseURL: srv.URL + \\\"/v1\\\",\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tAPIKey:  \\\"test\\\",\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tModel:   \\\"Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit\\\",\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t// Flavor \\\"omlx\\\" pins the capability table to the one used in\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t// production for vidar-omlx routing — matches the real\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t// dispatch path.\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tFlavor: \\\"omlx\\\",\\n+\\t\\t\\t})\\n+\\n+\\t\\t\\tr := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir(), TimeoutMS: 10000})\\n+\\t\\t\\tr.LookPath = mockLookPath\\n+\\t\\t\\tr.AgentProvider = provider\\n+\\n+\\t\\t\\t// A WorkDir prevents the agent from using the live worktree when\\n+\\t\\t\\t// it invokes its built-in read/write/bash tools during the loop.\\n+\\t\\t\\tresult, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tHarness: \\\"agent\\\",\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tPrompt:  \\\"smoke test — omlx fixture replay\\\",\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tWorkDir: t.TempDir(),\\n+\\t\\t\\t})\\n+\\t\\t\\trequire.NoError(t, err, \\\"RunAgent must not return a plumbing error; failures are reported via result.ExitCode / result.Error\\\")\\n+\\n+\\t\\t\\t// The regression signature is always disallowed — on BOTH paths.\\n+\\t\\t\\t// v0.3.13 would surface this even for well-formed streams when\\n+\\t\\t\\t// keep-alive comment frames were present.\\n+\\t\\t\\tif fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain != \\\"\\\" {\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.NotContains(t, result.Error, fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain,\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"consumer surfaced the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame regression signature — the sseCommentFilter in ddx-agent v0.3.14+ is missing or broken\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\n+\\t\\t\\tswitch fix.Meta.Expected.Status {\\n+\\t\\t\\tcase \\\"success\\\":\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.Equal(t, 0, result.ExitCode, \\\"happy-path fixture must close cleanly; got error=%q\\\", result.Error)\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.Empty(t, result.Error, \\\"happy-path must produce no error\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ContentContains; want != \\\"\\\" {\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.Contains(t, result.Output, want,\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"happy-path must assemble content from post-keep-alive data frames\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\t\\tcase \\\"error\\\":\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.Equal(t, 1, result.ExitCode, \\\"error-path fixture must report failure via ExitCode=1\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.NotEmpty(t, result.Error, \\\"error-path must surface a non-empty error\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorContains; want != \\\"\\\" {\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.Contains(t, result.Error, want,\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"error-path must propagate the underlying parse error\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"fixture %s: unknown expected status %q\\\", name, fix.Meta.Expected.Status)\\n+\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\t})\\n+\\t}\\n+}\\n+\\n+// TestOMLXFixtureReplay_FixturesExist is a cheap guardrail that fails fast if\\n+// the testdata/omlx-wire directory is emptied or renamed. The full replay test\\n+// above would also fail in that case, but a dedicated assertion surfaces the\\n+// structural problem without the noise of a streaming-test stack trace.\\n+func TestOMLXFixtureReplay_FixturesExist(t *testing.T) {\\n+\\tfor _, name := range []string{\\\"happy_path.jsonl\\\", \\\"error_path.jsonl\\\"} {\\n+\\t\\tp := filepath.Join(\\\"testdata\\\", \\\"omlx-wire\\\", name)\\n+\\t\\tinfo, err := os.Stat(p)\\n+\\t\\trequire.NoError(t, err, \\\"missing fixture %s\\\", p)\\n+\\t\\tassert.False(t, info.IsDir(), \\\"%s must be a file\\\", p)\\n+\\t\\tassert.NotZero(t, info.Size(), \\\"%s must not be empty\\\", p)\\n+\\t}\\n+}\\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go\\nnew file mode 100644\\nindex 00000000..fd43c76e\\n--- /dev/null\\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go\\n@@ -0,0 +1,66 @@\\n+//go:build omlx_smoke\\n+\\n+// Opt-in live smoke test for the ddx work → ddx-agent → vidar-omlx consumer\\n+// path. Unlike the fixture-replay test in omlx_e2e_test.go (which ships in CI\\n+// and uses a recorded test double), this test hits a real omlx endpoint and is\\n+// intended for manual release verification against a live server. It is gated\\n+// by the `omlx_smoke` build tag so the default `go test ./...` never runs it.\\n+//\\n+// Run:\\n+//\\n+//\\tOMLX_SMOKE_URL=http://vidar:1235/v1\\\\\\n+//\\tOMLX_SMOKE_MODEL=Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit\\\\\\n+//\\tgo test -tags omlx_smoke -run TestOMLXLiveSmoke -v ./cli/internal/agent\\n+//\\n+// This covers the same happy-path shape the CI fixture does, but against a\\n+// real server — useful for catching provider-side regressions (the omlx\\n+// server ships a breaking change, HTTP stack differences, headers, etc.) that\\n+// a recorded fixture cannot detect.\\n+\\n+package agent\\n+\\n+import
